text: Ṭā, Seen, Meem.
These are the verses of the clear Book.
Perhaps, [O Muḥammad], you would kill yourself with grief that they will not be believers.
tl: La'allaka baakhi'un nafsaka allaa yakoonoo mu'mineen

text: If We willed, We could send down to them from the sky a sign for which their necks would remain humbled.
tl: In nashaa nunazzil 'alaihim minas samaaa'i Aayatan fazallat a'naaquhum lahaa khaadi'een

text: And no mention [i.e., revelation] comes to them anew from the Most Merciful except that they turn away from it.
tl: Wa-maa yaateehim min zikrim minar Rahmaani muhdasin illaa kaanoo 'anhu mu'rideen

text: For they have already denied, but there will come to them the news of that which they used to ridicule.
tl: Faqad kazzaboo fasa-yaateehim ambaaa'u maa kaanoo bihee yastahzi'oon

text: Did they not look at the earth - how much We have produced therein from every noble kind?
tl: Awa-lam yaraw ilal ardi kam ambatnaa feehaa min kulli zawjin kareem

text: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
tl: Inna fee zaalika la-Aayah; Wa-maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
And [mention] when your Lord called Moses, [saying], \"Go to the wrongdoing people -
tl: Wa-iz naadaa Rabbuka Moosaaa ani' til qawmaz zaalimeen

text: The people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear Allāh?\"
He said, \"My Lord, indeed I fear that they will deny me
tl: Qaala Rabbi inneee akhaafu ai yukazziboon

text: And that my breast will tighten and my tongue will not be fluent, so send for Aaron.
tl: Wa-yadeequ sadree Wa-laa yantaliqu lisaanee fa-arsil ilaa Haaroon

text: And they have upon me a [claim due to] sin, so I fear that they will kill me.\"
[Allāh] said, \"No. Go both of you with Our signs; indeed, We are with you, listening.
tl: Qaala kallaa fazhabaa bi-Aayaatinaaa innaa ma'akum mustami'oon

text: Go to Pharaoh and say, 'We are the messengers of the Lord of the worlds,
tl: Faatiyaa Fir'awna faqoolaaa innaa Rasoolu Rabbil 'aalameen

text: [Commanded to say], \"Send with us the Children of Israel.\"'\"
[Pharaoh] said, \"Did we not raise you among us as a child, and you remained among us for years of your life?
tl: Qaala alam nurabbika feenaa waleedanw Wa-labista feenaa min 'umurika sineen

text: And [then] you did your deed which you did, and you were of the ungrateful.\"
tl: Wa-fa'alta fa'latakal latee fa'alta Wa-anta minal kaafireen

text: [Moses] said, \"I did it, then, while I was of those astray [i.e., ignorant].
tl: Qaala fa'altuhaaa izanw Wa-ana minad daaaleen

text: So I fled from you when I feared you. Then my Lord granted me judgement [i.e., wisdom and prophethood] and appointed me [as one] of the messengers.
tl: Fafarartu minkum lam-maa khiftukum fawahaba lee Rabbee hukmanw Wa-ja'alanee minal mursaleen

text: And is this a favor of which you remind me - that you have enslaved the Children of Israel?\"
tl: Wa-tilka ni'matun tamun-nuhaa 'alaiya an 'abbatta Baneee Israaa'eel

text: Said Pharaoh, \"And what is the Lord of the worlds?\"
[Moses] said, \"The Lord of the heavens and earth and that between them, if you should be convinced.\"
tl: Qaala Rabbus samaawaati wal-ardi Wa-maa bainahumaa in kuntum mooqineen

text: [Pharaoh] said to those around him, \"Do you not hear?\"
[Moses] said, \"Your Lord and the Lord of your first forefathers.\"
[Pharaoh] said, \"Indeed, your 'messenger' who has been sent to you is mad.\"
tl: Qaala inna Rasoolakumul lazee ursila ilaikum lamajnoon

text: [Moses] said, \"Lord of the east and the west and that between them, if you were to reason.\"
tl: Qaala Rabbul mashriqi wal-maghribi Wa-maa baina-humaa in kuntum ta'qiloon

text: [Pharaoh] said, \"If you take a god other than me, I will surely place you among those imprisoned.\"
tl: Qaala la'init takhazta ilaahan ghairee la-aj'alannaka minal masjooneen

text: [Moses] said, \"Even if I brought you something [i.e., proof] manifest?\"
[Pharaoh] said, \"Then bring it, if you should be of the truthful.\"
tl: Qaala faati biheee in kunta minas saadiqeen

text: So [Moses] threw his staff, and suddenly it was a serpent manifest.
And he drew out his hand; thereupon it was white for the observers.
tl: Wa-naza'a yadahoo faizaa hiya baidaaa'u linnaa-zireen

text: [Pharaoh] said to the eminent ones around him, \"Indeed, this is a learned magician.
tl: Qaala lilmala-i hawlahooo inna haazaa lasaahirun 'aleem

text: He wants to drive you out of your land by his magic, so what do you advise?\"
tl: Yureedu aien yukhrijakum min ardikum bisihrihee famaazaa taamuroon

text: They said, \"Postpone [the matter of] him and his brother and send among the cities gatherers
tl: Qaalooo arjih Wa-akhaahu wab'as fil madaaa'ini haashireen

text: Who will bring you every learned, skilled magician.\"
So the magicians were assembled for the appointment of a well-known day.
And it was said to the people, \"Will you congregate
That we might follow the magicians if they are the predominant?\"
tl: La'allanaa nattabi'us saharata in kaanoo humul ghaalibeen

text: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, \"Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?\"
tl: Falammaa jaaa'as saharatu qaaloo li-Fir'awna a'inna lanaa la-ajjran in kunnaa nahnul ghaalibeen

text: He said, \"Yes, and indeed, you will then be of those near [to me].\"
tl: Qaala na'am Wa-innakum izal laminal muqarrabeen

text: Moses said to them, \"Throw whatever you will throw.\"
tl: Qaala lahum Moosaaa alqoo maaa antum mulqoon

text: So they threw their ropes and their staffs and said, \"By the might of Pharaoh, indeed it is we who are predominant.\"
tl: Fa-alqaw hibaalahum Wa-'isiyyahum Wa-qaaloo bi'izzati Fir'awna innaa lanahnul ghaaliboon

text: Then Moses threw his staff, and at once it devoured what they falsified.
tl: Fa-alqaa Moosaa 'asaahu fa-izaa hiya talqafu maa yaafikoon

text: So the magicians fell down in prostration [to Allāh].
They said, \"We have believed in the Lord of the worlds,
The Lord of Moses and Aaron.\"

[Pharaoh] said, \"You believed him [i.e., Moses] before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic, but you are going to know. I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you all.\"
tl: Qaala aamantum lahoo qabla an aazana lakum innahoo lakabeerukumul lazee 'alla-makumus sihra falasawfa ta'lamoon; la-uqatti'anna aidiyakum Wa-arjulakum min khilaafinw Wa-la-usallibanna-kum ajma'een

text: They said, \"No harm. Indeed, to our Lord we will return.
tl: Qaaloo la daira innaaa ilaa Rabbinaa munqalliboon

text: Indeed, we aspire that our Lord will forgive us our sins because we were the first of the believers.\"
tl: Innaa natma'u aien yaghfira lanaa Rabbunna khataa-yaanaaa an kunnaaa awwalal mu'mineen

text: And We inspired to Moses, \"Travel by night with My servants; indeed, you will be pursued.\"
tl: Wa-awhainaaa ilaa Moosaaa an asri bi'ibaadeee innakum muttaba'oon

text: Then Pharaoh sent among the cities gatherers
[And said], \"Indeed, those are but a small band,
And indeed, they are enraging us,
And indeed, we are a cautious society...\"
So We removed them from gardens and springs
And treasures and honorable station -
Thus. And We caused to inherit it the Children of Israel.
So they pursued them at sunrise.
And when the two companies saw one another, the companions of Moses said, \"Indeed, we are to be overtaken!\"
tl: Falammaa taraaa'al jam'aani qaala as-haabu Moosaaa innaa lamudrakoon

text: [Moses] said, \"No! Indeed, with me is my Lord; He will guide me.\"
tl: Qaala kallaaa inna ma'iya Rabbee sa-yahdeen

text: Then We inspired to Moses, \"Strike with your staff the sea,\" and it parted, and each portion was like a great towering mountain.
tl: Fa-awhainaaa ilaa Moosaaa anid rib bi'asaakal bahra fanfalaqa fakaana kullu firqin kattawdil 'azeem

text: And We advanced thereto the others [i.e., the pursuers].
And We saved Moses and those with him, all together.
Then We drowned the others.
Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
tl: Inna fee zaalika la-Aayaah; Wa-maa kaana aksaru-hu mu'mineen

text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
And recite to them the news of Abraham,
When he said to his father and his people, \"What do you worship?\"
tl: Iz qaala li-abeehi Wa-qawmihee maa ta'budoon

text: They said, \"We worship idols and remain to them devoted.\"
He said, \"Do they hear you when you supplicate?
Or do they benefit you, or do they harm?\"
They said, \"But we found our fathers doing thus.\"
tl: Qaaloo bal wajadnaaa aabaaa-'anaa kazaalika yaf'aloon

text: He said, \"Then do you see what you have been worshipping,
You and your ancient forefathers?
Indeed, they are enemies to me, except the Lord of the worlds,
Who created me, and He [it is who] guides me.
And it is He who feeds me and gives me drink.
And when I am ill, it is He who cures me
And who will cause me to die and then bring me to life
And who I aspire that He will forgive me my sin on the Day of Recompense.\"
tl: Wallazeee atma'u aien yaghfira lee khateee'-atee Yawmad Deen

text: [And he said], \"My Lord, grant me authority and join me with the righteous.
tl: Rabbi hab lee hukmanw Wa-alhiqnee bis-saaliheen

text: And grant me a mention [i.e., reputation] of honor among later generations.
tl: Waj'al lee lisaana sidqin fil aakhireen

text: And place me among the inheritors of the Garden of Pleasure.
And forgive my father. Indeed, he has been of those astray.
tl: Waghfir li-abeee innahoo kaana mind daalleen

text: And do not disgrace me on the Day they are [all] resurrected -
The Day when there will not benefit [anyone] wealth or children
tl: Yawma laa yanfa'u maalunw Wa-laa banoon

text: But only one who comes to Allāh with a sound heart.\"
tl: Illaa man atal laaha biqalbin saleem

text: And Paradise will be brought near [that Day] to the righteous.
And Hellfire will be brought forth for the deviators,
And it will be said to them, \"Where are those you used to worship
tl: Wa-qeela lahum aina maa kuntum ta'budoon

text: Other than Allāh? Can they help you or help themselves?\"
tl: Min doonil laahi hal yansuroonakum aw yantasiroon

text: So they will be overturned into it [i.e., Hellfire], they and the deviators
And the soldiers of Iblees, all together.
They will say while they dispute therein,
\"By Allāh, we were indeed in manifest error
tl: Tallaahi in kunnaa lafee dalaalim mubeen

text: When we equated you with the Lord of the worlds.
And no one misguided us except the criminals.
So now we have no intercessors
And not a devoted friend.
Then if we only had a return [to the world] and could be of the believers...\"
tl: Falaw anna lanaa karratan fanakoona minal mu'mineen

text: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
tl: Inna fee zaalika la-Aayatanw Wa-maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
The people of Noah denied the messengers
When their brother Noah said to them, \"Will you not fear Allāh?
tl: Iz qaala lahum akhoohum Noohun alaa tattaqoon

text: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
So fear Allāh and obey me.
And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
tl: Wa-maaa as'alukum 'alaihi min ajrin in ajriya illaa 'alaa Rabbil 'aalameen

text: So fear Allāh and obey me.\"
They said, \"Should we believe you while you are followed by the lowest [class of people]?\"
He said, \"And what is my knowledge of what they used to do?
Their account is only upon my Lord, if you [could] perceive.
tl: In hisaabuhum illaa 'alaa Rabbee law tash'uroon

text: And I am not one to drive away the believers.
I am only a clear warner.\"
They said, \"If you do not desist, O Noah, you will surely be of those who are stoned.\"
tl: Qaaloo la'il lam tantahi yaa-Noohu latakoonanna minal marjoomeen

text: He said, \"My Lord, indeed my people have denied me.
Then judge between me and them with decisive judgement and save me and those with me of the believers.\"
tl: Faftab bainee Wa-bai-nahum fat-hanw Wa-najjinee Wa-mam ma'iya minal mu'mineen

text: So We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.
tl: Fa-anjainaahu Wa-mam ma'ahoo fil fulkil mashhoon

text: Then We drowned thereafter the remaining ones.
Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
tl: Inna fee zaalika la-Aayaah; Wa-maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
ʿAad denied the messengers
When their brother Hūd said to them, \"Will you not fear Allāh?
tl: Iz qaala lahum akhoohum Hoodun alaa tattaqoon

text: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
So fear Allāh and obey me.
And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
tl: Wa-maa as'alukum 'alaihi min ajrin in ajriya illaa 'alaa Rabbil 'aalameen

text: Do you construct on every elevation a sign, amusing yourselves,
And take for yourselves constructions [i.e., palaces and fortresses] that you might abide eternally?
And when you strike, you strike as tyrants.
So fear Allāh and obey me.
And fear He who provided you with that which you know,
Provided you with grazing livestock and children
And gardens and springs.
Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a terrible day.\"
They said, \"It is all the same to us whether you advise or are not of the advisors.
tl: Qaaloo sawaaa'un 'alainaaa awa-'azta am lam takum minal waa'izeen

text: This is not but the custom of the former peoples,
And we are not to be punished.\"
And they denied him, so We destroyed them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
tl: Fakazzaboohu fa-ahlaknaahum; inna fee zaalika la-aayah; Wa-maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
Thamūd denied the messengers
When their brother Ṣāliḥ said to them, \"Will you not fear Allāh?
tl: Iz qaala lahum akhoohum Saalihun alaa tattaqoon

text: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
So fear Allāh and obey me.
And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
tl: Wa-maaa as'alukum 'alaihi min ajrin in ajriya illaa 'alaa Rabbil 'aalameen

text: Will you be left in what is here, secure [from death],
Within gardens and springs
And fields of crops and palm trees with softened fruit?
And you carve out of the mountains, homes, with skill.
So fear Allāh and obey me.
And do not obey the order of the transgressors,
Who cause corruption in the land and do not amend.\"
tl: Allazeena yufsidoona fil ardi Wa-laa yuslihoon

text: They said, \"You are only of those affected by magic.
You are but a man like ourselves, so bring a sign, if you should be of the truthful.\"
tl: Maaa anta illaa basharum mislunaa faati bi-Aayatin in kunta minas saadiqeen

text: He said, \"This is a she-camel. For her is a [time of] drink, and for you is a [time of] drink, [each] on a known day.
tl: Qaala haazihee naaqatul lahaa shirbunw walakum shirbu yawmim ma'loom

text: And do not touch her with harm, lest you be seized by the punishment of a terrible day.\"
But they hamstrung her and so became regretful.
And the punishment seized them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
tl: Fa-akhazahumul 'azaab; inna fee zaalika la-Aayah; Wa-maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
The people of Lot denied the messengers
When their brother Lot said to them, \"Will you not fear Allāh?
tl: Iz qaala lahum akhoohum Lootun alaa tattaqoon

text: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
So fear Allāh and obey me.
And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
tl: Wa-maaa as'alukum 'alaihi min ajrin in ajriya illaa 'alaa Rabbil 'aalameen

text: Do you approach males among the worlds
And leave what your Lord has created for you as mates? But you are a people transgressing.\"
tl: Wa-tazaroona maa khalaqa lakum Rabbukum min azwaajikum; bal antum qawmun 'aadoon

text: They said, \"If you do not desist, O Lot, you will surely be of those evicted.\"
tl: Qaloo la'il lam tantahi yaa-Lootu latakoonanna minal mukhrajeen

text: He said, \"Indeed, I am, toward your deed, of those who detest [it].
My Lord, save me and my family from [the consequence of] what they do.\"
So We saved him and his family, all,
Except an old woman among those who remained behind.
Then We destroyed the others.
And We rained upon them a rain [of stones], and evil was the rain of those who were warned.
Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
tl: Inna fee zaalika la-Aayah; Wa-maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
The companions of the thicket [i.e., the people of Madyan] denied the messengers
When Shuʿayb said to them, \"Will you not fear Allāh?
tl: Iz qaala lahum Shu'aybun alaa tattaqoon

text: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
So fear Allāh and obey me.
And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
tl: Wa-maaa as'alukum 'alaihi min ajrin in ajriya illaa 'alaa Rabbil 'aalameen

text: Give full measure and do not be of those who cause loss.
tl: Awful kaila Wa-laa takoonoo minal mukhsireen

text: And weigh with an even [i.e., honest] balance.
And do not deprive people of their due and do not commit abuse on earth, spreading corruption.
tl: Wa-laa tabkhasun naasa ashyaaa-'ahum Wa-laa ta'saw fil ardi mufsideen

text: And fear He who created you and the former creation.\"
They said, \"You are only of those affected by magic.
You are but a man like ourselves, and indeed, we think you are among the liars.
tl: Wa-maaa anta illaa basharum mislunaa Wa-in nazunnuka laminal kaazibeen

text: So cause to fall upon us fragments of the sky, if you should be of the truthful.\"
tl: Fa-asqit 'alainaa kisafam minas samaaa'i in kunta minas saadiqeen

text: He said, \"My Lord is most knowing of what you do.\"
And they denied him, so the punishment of the day of the black cloud seized them. Indeed, it was the punishment of a terrible day.
tl: Fakazzaboohu fa-akhazahum 'azaabu Yawmiz zullah; innahoo kaana 'azaaba Yawmin 'Azeem

text: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
tl: Inna fee zaalika la-Aayah; Wa-maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
And indeed, it [i.e., the Qur’ān] is the revelation of the Lord of the worlds.
The Trustworthy Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] has brought it down
Upon your heart, [O Muḥammad] - that you may be of the warners
In a clear Arabic language.
And indeed, it is [mentioned] in the scriptures of former peoples.
And has it not been a sign to them that it is recognized by the scholars of the Children of Israel?
tl: Awalam yakul lahum Aayatan ai ya'lamahoo 'ulamaaa'u Baneee Israaa'eel

text: And even if We had revealed it to one among the foreigners
And he had recited it to them [perfectly], they would [still] not have been believers in it.
Thus have We inserted it [i.e., disbelief] into the hearts of the criminals.
They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment.
tl: Laa yu'minoona bihee hattaa yarawul 'azaabal aleem

text: And it will come to them suddenly while they perceive [it] not.
And they will say, \"May we be reprieved?\"
So for Our punishment are they impatient?
Then have you considered if We gave them enjoyment for years
And then there came to them that which they were promised?
They would not be availed by the enjoyment with which they were provided.
And We did not destroy any city except that it had warners
tl: Wa-maaa ahlaknaa min qaryatin illaa lahaa munziroon

text: As a reminder; and never have We been unjust.
And the devils have not brought it [i.e., the revelation] down.
It is not allowable for them, nor would they be able.
Indeed they, from [its] hearing, are removed.
So do not invoke with Allāh another deity and [thus] be among the punished.
tl: Falaa tad'u ma'al laahi ilaahan aakhara fatakoona minal mu'azzabeen

text: And warn, [O Muḥammad], your closest kindred.
And lower your wing [i.e., show kindness] to those who follow you of the believers.
tl: Wakhfid janaahaka limanit taba-'aka minal mu'mineen

text: And if they disobey you, then say, \"Indeed, I am disassociated from what you are doing.\"
tl: Fa-in asawka faqul innee bareee'um mimmmaa ta'maloon

text: And rely upon the Exalted in Might, the Merciful,
Who sees you when you arise
And your movement among those who prostrate.
Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
Shall I inform you upon whom the devils descend?
They descend upon every sinful liar.
They pass on what is heard, and most of them are liars.
And the poets - [only] the deviators follow them.
Do you not see that in every valley they roam
tl: Alam tara annahum fee kulli waadiny yaheemoon

text: And that they say what they do not do? -

Except those [poets] who believe and do righteous deeds and remember Allāh often and defend [the Muslims] after they were wronged. And those who have wronged are going to know to what [kind of] return they will be returned.
tl: Illal lazeena aamanoo Wa-'amilus saalihaati Wa-zakarul laaha kaseeranw wantasaroo min ba'di maa zulimoo; Wa-saya'lamul lazeena zalamooo aiya munqalabiny yanqaliboon
